intelligence.hypothesis: Insufficient telemetry; awaiting next valid capture.

vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely unsupported by the visual evidence. P1 (String of Nickels) is absent. P2 (Mexican Mint) and P4 (Silver Guest) are not identifiable as distinct specimens; only a single, struggling sprout is visible. P3 (Pothos) is represented only by the white rabbit anchor, with no foliage present.

plant_audit.P1_Yellow_Pot: Empty soil surface; no botanical presence detected.
plant_audit.P2_P4_Black_Pot: Contains a single, isolated green sprout. No evidence of Mexican Mint or Silver Guest characteristics.
plant_audit.P3_Black_Pot: Contains only the white rabbit anchor; no Pothos foliage observed.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Consistently dark, appears damp/saturated across all pots.
biome_observations.surface_debris: Eggshell fragments present in the central black pot, acting as a potential soil amendment or debris.
biome_observations.desk_environment: Fixed cabling and white rabbit anchor are the only stable elements.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t4: Initial state shows a single green sprout in the central pot.
temporal_deltas.t4_to_t2: Sprout shows slight postural change, leaning toward the light source.
temporal_deltas.t2_to_current: The sprout remains in a state of stasis; no new growth or leaf development observed.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of severe decline. The lack of expected foliage suggests a failure of the primary specimens. The single remaining sprout is the only sign of life, but it lacks the vigor of a healthy plant, indicating potential root rot or insufficient light/nutrient uptake.
anomalies: Eggshell fragments in the central pot (New Intervention).
Absence of all registered plant species (Systemic Loss).
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical audit reveals a critical failure of the indoor garden registry. The pots are largely devoid of their intended occupants. The only biological activity is a single, solitary sprout in the central black pot. The environment appears to be suffering from a lack of proper light or potential over-saturation of the soil, as evidenced by the dark, damp appearance of the substrate. The white rabbit anchor remains the only constant in an otherwise depleted biome.
